title: Handle missing sync directory on startup
changes: Create `WatcherError` enum with case `directoryNotFound(expected: URL, message: String)`
In `startMonitoring()`, before snapshotting or creating the FSEvents stream, check `FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: directory.path)`

If the directory does not exist, log an error at `.error` level: "Voice Memos sync directory not found at \(directory.path). Is iCloud configured and has Voice Memos been used at least once?" and throw `WatcherError.directoryNotFound`
The caller (eventually the orchestrator) decides how to handle this — the watcher does not crash, retry, or poll for the directory to appear

tests.name: test_throwsWhenDirectoryMissing
tests.description: Init the watcher with a non-existent directory path. Call startMonitoring(). Assert it throws WatcherError.directoryNotFound with the expected path in the error.

tests.name: test_errorMessageIncludesExpectedPath
tests.description: Catch the error, assert the expected URL matches the path passed to init.

doneWhen: Starting the watcher against a non-existent directory throws a descriptive error instead of crashing. The process remains alive. Tests pass.
doNot: Automatically create the directory — it's managed by iCloud, not by Utterd
Implement retry or polling for the directory to appear — keep the watcher simple
Catch the error inside the watcher — let it propagate to the caller
